title: Lightly Fermented Vietnamese Beef Pho Bo
description: A fermented, tender, and energizing Vietnamese dish featuring treacle and an al-dente finish, with Mediterranean-friendly flavors, in the form of pho bo.

ingredients.name: beef bones
ingredients.notes: preferably oxtail or beef neck bones

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roast the beef bones in the oven for 30 minutes to enhance the flavor.
In a large pot, combine the roasted beef bones, fish sauce, treacle, onion, ginger, star anise, cinnamon sticks, and black cardamom.
Add 4 quarts of water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Let the broth simmer for 1 hour to allow the flavors to meld together.
While the broth is simmering, cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions until they are al-dente.
Slice the beef brisket into thin strips and add it to the pot, cooking for an additional 10 minutes or until the beef is tender.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the cooked noodles, bean sprouts, and sliced beef to the pot, cooking for an additional 2 minutes to heat through.
Serve the pho bo hot, garnished with lime wedges and any desired toppings.
